data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang Doesn't Expect a Major Impact from Tariffs
data.description:
Shares of chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) are down today despite CEO Jensen Huang saying that he doesn't expect potential tariffs to have a major impact on the company's financial outlook. Indeed, Huang explained that Nvidia's supply chain is flexible and spread across several countries, which helps reduce the risk of disruptions. Even if tariffs are imposed, the company can adjust based on where products are built, purchased, and delivered.

Unsurprisingly, President Trump has proposed tariffs of up to 25% on chips made outside of the U.S., which could affect companies like Nvidia, AMD (AMD), and Apple (AAPL). Huang said that although many of Nvidia's chips are made by TSMC (TSM) in Taiwan, the company is working to increase its U.S.-based manufacturing. He believes that by adding domestic production by the end of the year, Nvidia will be in a stronger position to manage future trade risks.

Interestingly, TSMC also recently announced that it will increase its U.S. investment to $165 billion in order to build more chip plants and facilities. However, it is worth noting that TSMC has many customers, so Nvidia may not be able to get all the capacity it needs. Along with trade concerns, Nvidia is also facing tighter U.S. export controls on chips going into China. This comes after Chinese firm DeepSeek created powerful AI models using Nvidia's mid-range chips, which raised concerns that China could gain an edge in AI development.

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, analysts remain bullish on NVDA stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $176.54 per share implies an upside potential of 51.7% from current levels.

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: IonQ (IONQ) Board Member Buys $2 Million of Company Stock
data.description:
A board member of quantum computing firm IonQ (IONQ) has made a large purchase of company stock as the share price has fallen.

IONQ stock is down nearly 50% this year amid concerns about the viability and future development of quantum computers. However, at least one of the company’s board members, has used the pullback to buy more company stock.

IonQ board director Bill Scannell paid $2 million on March 12 to purchase 93,066 IONQ shares at an average price of $21.81 each. He now owns 135,047 IonQ shares in a personal account, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Buying the Dip

In addition to his role at IonQ, Scannell serves as president of global sales and customer operations at Dell Technologies (DELL). He joined IonQ's board of directors in February 2024, and this was his first purchase of IONQ stock on the open market. It was also the first open-market buy of IONQ stock by a company insider this year.

The downturn in IonQ’s share price over recent months comes after the stock more than tripled in 2024 as investors bet big on quantum computing. Alphabet (GOOGL) announced a quantum breakthrough last December that helped drive excitement for stocks of quantum computing companies that also include Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and D-Wave Quantum Systems (QBTS).

Quantum computing stocks took a hit earlier this year after Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said that quantum computing wouldn't be a useful technology for several more decades. However, Huang just walked back those comments and said he was “wrong” about the timing related to quantum computers.

Is IONQ Stock a Buy?

The stock of IonQ has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among six Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy and two Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average IONQ price target of $41.60 implies 89.35% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on IONQ stock

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Rallies after Musk Tells Employees to Hold onto Their Shares
data.description:
Shares of EV maker Tesla (TSLA) are rallying after CEO Elon Musk held a company-wide meeting in order to improve morale amid the stock’s 50% decline. Speaking during a livestream on X, Musk joked that the media makes things seem worse than they are and said he sees Teslas on fire every time he passes a TV. He also encouraged employees to stay hopeful and hold onto their shares despite sales and deliveries dropping in key markets and protests due to Musk's connection to the Trump administration.

Unsurprisingly, Musk repeated his long-standing promise that Teslas will be able to drive themselves soon with a software update, though he admitted that those plans have taken longer than expected. In addition, he said that the Model Y is still the best-selling car in the world, even though production was recently paused to make updates. Tesla also plans to release cheaper models this year but hasn't shared many details.

It is worth mentioning that the meeting was unusual because Tesla doesn't usually hold public all-hands events. Interestingly, Musk answered employee questions for about 30 minutes, including one about whether he'd ever build planes or trains. He said he would love to make an electric airplane someday but is too busy right now. Analyst Dan Ives, who usually supports Musk, said the meeting was a good first step but warned that Tesla is in a tough spot and needs Musk to focus more on the company.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 14 Buys, 11 Holds, and 11 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $335.32 per share implies 38.9% upside potential.

See more TSLA analyst ratings

data.title: Does Elon Know? Secret Service Chief Meets General Motors (NYSE:GM) on Next-Generation Armored SUVs
data.description:
Donald Trump may be a Tesla (TSLA) owner these days, but the Secret Service is very much sticking with Detroit. The head of the U.S. Secret Service was in Michigan to meet executives from General Motors (GM) this week to discuss the next generation of armored SUVs, the agency said on Friday, just a day after Tesla was forced to recall virtually all its Cybertrucks due to a build quality flaw. I guess you call it “timing”.

Secret Service Sticking with GM

The Secret Service posted on X that Director Sean Curran met with GM executives "to discuss advancements that could benefit the next generation of armored SUVs." 

In November 2023 GM won a ten-year, $300 million contract to deliver the next generation Heavy-Duty armoured Sport Utility Vehicle (HD SUV) in support of the State Department's Diplomatic Security Service. These are basically armored Chevy Suburbans featuring a specially-designed body-on-frame chassis and suspension.  

GM completed the first production vehicle in October. Steve duMont, GM Defense president, said the vehicles have "good acceleration, handled better in the turns and the heavy-duty anti-lock braking system was far superior to our aftermarket vehicles." 

In January the company said its GM Defense subsidiary is providing these next generation Suburban Shield armored vehicles to the Qatar Armed Forces as well. 

"Countering evolving threats require us to constantly explore new innovations and improvements to our armored fleet of protective vehicles," the Secret Service added in its Friday post on X. 

In September, the Secret Service along with the the Homeland Security Department awarded GM a $14.8 million contract to develop the next-generation presidential limousine, which could be worth up to $40.8 million through 2029. The heavily armored presidential limousine, known as The Beast, is a custom-built Cadillac from GM. Meanwhile in October GM unveiled its latest next-generation tactical vehicle prototype, based on the Chevrolet Silverado 3500HD ZR2 truck.

It comes amid all kinds of problems for Tesla’s Cybertruck after nearly all were recalled due to potential issue where an exterior panel may detach while driving.

Last month, Tesla CEO Musk denied reports that the State Department was planning to buy $400 million of "Armored Tesla" later this year, after details about a planned purchase of "Tesla Armored Electric Vehicles" cropped up in a 2025 procurement report.

Is GM a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, GM has a Moderate Buy rating based on ten Buys, four Holds and one Sell. The average GM price target of $63.53 implies about 27% upside.

See more GM analyst ratings

data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: CVX, XOM, OXY: Trump Administration Expands Oil Drilling in Alaska
data.description:
The administration of U.S. President Donald Trump has announced plans to expand drilling for crude oil and natural gas within the Alaskan wilderness.

The U.S. Department of the Interior has begun implementing plans to expand energy and mineral resource production in Alaska. President Trump has promised to “unleash Alaska’s extraordinary resource potential” and was elected on a promise to let oil and natural gas companies “drill, baby, drill.”

The Interior Department and Bureau of Land Management are reopening 82% of the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska to leasing and energy project development. They also plan to open the entire Coastal Plain of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge to oil leases and will restart the Alaska LNG Project.

The expansion of Alaska territory to energy development is seen as positive news for leading oil and natural gas companies such as Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental Petroleum (OXY).

Environmental Criticism

The actions kickstart a regulatory review process that includes lengthy comment periods, as well as proposed and final rules by the U.S. federal government. Environmental organizations were quick to criticize the move, with the Sierra Club saying, “Donald Trump believes Big Oil CEOs, billionaires, and corporate polluters should benefit from America’s public lands, not we the people.”

The announcement came a day after Trump met at the White House with the American Petroleum Institute and the heads of the largest U.S. oil companies. Previous U.S. President Joe Biden had banned drilling on 28 million acres of Alaskan federal lands. Trump is moving to reverse that ban.

The 23-million-acre National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska is estimated to hold 895 million barrels of crude oil. There is also believed to be $44 billion worth of natural gas in the Alaska reserve area.

Is CVX Stock a Buy?

The stock of Chevron, one of the leading U.S. oil majors, has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the past three months. The average CVX price target of $176.64 implies 7.32% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on CVX stock

data.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Headed for Its Worst First Quarter Performance Since 2020
data.description:
As we head towards the final trading week of March, Bitcoin (BTC) is on pace for its worst first quarter performance since 2020 when the Covid-19 pandemic hit global markets.

So far in the first quarter, Bitcoin has declined 10%, putting it into a correction and marking its worst Q1 performance in five years. At the same time, Ethereum (ETH), the second largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, has posted its weakest first quarter performance since its inception in 2015.

The poor showings for both Bitcoin and Ethereum come as investors retreat from risk assets such as cryptocurrencies and stocks. Markets remain weak and volatile as U.S. President Donald Trump continues to push trade policies that include import tariffs, and as concerns rise about a U.S. recession.

Continued Volatility

Analysts say investors should expect increased volatility in crypto prices as we approach April 1 and traders look to rebalance their portfolios heading into the year's second quarter. Weakness in crypto has also been exacerbated by investor disappointment over President Trump's policies.

Many investors have been letdown by Trump’s lack of a concrete plans for a strategic Bitcoin reserve and a tax-free crypto capital gains policy. Bitcoin is currently trading at $84,000, down nearly 25% from an all-time high of just over $109,000 reached on Jan. 20, the day of Trump's inauguration.

Is BTC a Buy?

Most Wall Street firms don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin, so we'll look at the cryptocurrency's three-month performance instead. As one can see in the chart below, the price of BTC has declined 11.23% in the last 12 weeks.

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Nike's (NKE) Q3 Results Draw Mixed Reactions from Analysts
data.description:
Athletic apparel and footwear giant Nike (NKE) reported a decline in its revenue and earnings for the third quarter of Fiscal 2025 but managed to beat analysts' expectations. However, the company's outlook was worse than feared, with Q4 FY25 sales expected to decline at the low end of the mid-teens range. Nike stock was down 7% in Friday's pre-market trading. Analysts gave mixed reactions to Nike's results and a slower-than-anticipated turnaround.

Analysts Give Mixed Reactions to Nike's Results

Following the Q3 FY25 print, Citi analyst Paul Lejuez reiterated a Hold rating on Nike stock with a price target of $72. Lejuez noted that while Nike beat Q3 EPS estimates driven by better-than-anticipated sales, lower selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) expenses, and taxes, the company's gross margin lagged expectations. He added that management indicated that Q4 will be a low point for both sales and gross margin trajectory.

Lejuez highlighted that inventories are too high in all regions. Also, the analyst thinks that the notable weakness in Nike's China business indicates that this key market will take the longest to recover. The 5-star analyst stated that it is clear that management's "Win Now" strategy will take time. With Fiscal 2026 expected to be another year of lower sales and earnings, Lejuez contends that $3.00 in EPS isn't likely until Fiscal 2028, implying that Nike doesn't look to be an attractive stock at a P/E of 24x Fiscal 2028 earnings.

Likewise, Stifel analyst Jim Duffy reiterated a Hold rating on NKE stock with a price target of $75. The analyst noted that Nike's outlook for the second half of Fiscal 2025 remains unchanged, with stronger-than-expected Q3 FY25 results offset by Q4 guidance. The four-star analyst added that while Nike's marketplace cleanup is on track, he expects a return to healthy growth to take several quarters before positive revenue inflection and SG&A leverage are achieved. Duffy also noted persistent challenges in Greater China. Overall, Duffy contends that while Nike's market-leading position and performance-oriented products fuel optimism, he doesn't see a risk-adjusted upside case from current levels.

Meanwhile, Jefferies analyst Randal Konik reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $115. Konik noted that Nike's new products are already showing green shoots. He added that the company’s efforts to clean the inventory are on track, and it is rapidly rebuilding its relationships with wholesale partners. While Konik acknowledged that Nike's turnaround journey will take two years, he expects a V-shaped recovery in Fiscal 2027. With NKE stock trading at 10-year lows on a price-to-sales valuation basis, Konik says, "Just Buy It!”

Is NKE Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Nike stock based on 16 Buys and 12 Holds. The average NKE stock price target of $88.29 implies 23% upside potential.

It is important to note that analysts' ratings and price targets are subject to changes as more analysts are expected to react.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/21/25 – Futures Dip After Thursday's Market Losses
data.description:
U.S. stock futures declined slightly on Friday morning after all major indices closed lower yesterday. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.48%, 0.44%, and 0.42%, respectively, at 4:40 a.m. EST, March 21.

In Thursday’s trading session, the S&P 500, the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ), and the Dow Jones declined 0.2%, 0.3%, and 0.03%, respectively.

Investor concerns stem from the Federal Reserve’s recent policy announcements. While the Fed reaffirmed its forecast for two rate cuts this year, it also raised its inflation outlook and expects slower economic growth. This mix is causing fears of “stagflation,” which means slow growth and rising prices.

Adding to market uncertainty are President Trump’s proposed tariff policies, which Fed Chair Jerome Powell noted could impede progress on inflation.

Meanwhile, the Nasdaq remains in correction territory, down more than 10% from its recent peak.

In after-hours trading, Nike (NKE) and FedEx (FDX) stocks fell after reporting disappointing quarterly results. However, Micron Technology (MU) was in the green after it reported strong Q2 results.

Today, investors await results from some major companies such as Nio (NIO) and Carnival (CCL).

At the time of writing, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was up, floating near 4.239%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $68.32 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened lower today as investors assessed recent monetary policy decisions from central banks in Europe and the U.S.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Friday

Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today as traders reacted to U.S. economic data and were worried over Trump's tariffs. The upcoming April 2 deadline for reciprocal tariffs has added to global trade fears.

At the same time, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was down 2.19%. Further, China's Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices declined 1.29% and 1.76%, respectively. Also, Japan's Nikkei index was down 0.2%, while the Topix index was up 0.29%.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

Disclosure

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Precigen Stock (PGEN) Amid Positive Pipeline Developments
data.description:
Precigen (PGEN) reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings and is making strides toward commercializing its lead asset, PRGN-2012, for recurrent respiratory papillomatosis. The FDA has granted priority review to the company’s Biologics License Application (BLA) for PRGN-2012, with a decision expected by August 2025. Financially, while the company has faced increased research and development costs linked to PRGN-2012, it ended 2024 with a cash position that extends its runway into 2026. Analysts maintain a positive outlook on the stock, citing its promising pipeline and readiness for a prospective product launch.

Promising Pipeline Developments

Precigen is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing advanced gene and cell therapies. The company applies precision technology to tackle challenging diseases, particularly in immuno-oncology, autoimmune disorders, and infectious diseases. Precigen is advancing a diverse portfolio of unique therapies through preclinical and clinical stages, aiming to achieve clinical proof-of-concept and eventual commercialization.

The recent developments involving PRGN-2012, aimed at treating recurrent respiratory papillomatosis, mark a substantial step forward in the treatment's clinical progression. Precigen’s BLA has been accepted by the FDA without needing an advisory committee meeting, indicating a smooth path toward approval. This treatment has the potential to become the first therapy of its kind to gain FDA approval, thanks to its strong efficacy, safety profile, and ease of administration.

In preparation for a potential 2025 commercial launch, Precigen is actively advancing its commercial and manufacturing readiness. Financially, the Company is in a stable position with $97.9 million in cash and investments, which is projected to support its operations well into 2026, even beyond the anticipated introduction of PRGN-2012 to the market.

For the fiscal year 2024, the company faced a challenging financial landscape, with total revenue decreasing by 37%, or $2.3 million, primarily due to a reduction in product and service volumes at Exemplar. While the company marked a decrease in costs for products and services, it increased spending in research and development, which rose by $4.5 million due to clinical trials, manufacturing material costs, and commercial readiness planning. This contributed to the net loss widening to $126.2 million.

Analysts are Bullish

Analysts following the company remain bullish on its prospects. For instance, H.C. Wainwright analyst Swayampakula Ramakanth and JMP Securities analyst Jason Butler both maintain a Buy rating on shares of PGEN with a price target of $6.00 due to recent promising developments.  Clinical trial successes, particularly with PRGN-2012, and ongoing developments in its pipeline, such as PRGN-2009 and PRGN-3006, reinforce confidence in its growth potential. These factors, along with strategic facility upgrades and partnerships, support an optimistic outlook for revenue growth.

Precigen is rated a Strong Buy overall, based on the recent recommendations of three analysts. The average price target for PGEN stock is $6.33, which represents a potential upside of 251.67% from current levels.

Disclosure

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Casey Dylan, CIMA

data.title: Accenture (ACN) Warns of Revenue Hit as Musk's DOGE Cuts Bite, Analysts Slash Targets
data.description:
Global consulting firm Accenture (ACN) has warned of significant revenue risks due to budget cuts under the Elon Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). In its post-earnings call, Accenture stated that the DOGE's cost-cutting measures have led to delays and cancellations of its new contracts, impacting its sales. ACN stock dropped over 7% on Thursday as concerns about reduced government spending and a weak 2025 guidance overshadowed the positive Q2 results.

In context, DOGE is a cost-cutting initiative under Trump's second term that aims to cut federal spending and downsize the workforce.

Accenture Faces Headwinds

Accenture is among the first to highlight the revenue impact of DOGE's tightened spending, citing slowed procurement processes.

Moreover, the company stated that the new contracts with the U.S. government have significantly declined since President Trump took office in January. A recent federal filing revealed that an Accenture contract, which could have generated up to $5 million in additional business by 2027, was abruptly terminated. This marks the tenth Accenture contract or subcontract canceled under the Trump administration.

Notably, federal contracts accounted for about 8% of its global revenue and 16% of its revenue in the U.S. in FY24.

Wall Street Analysts Cut Price Targets on ACN Stock

Amid growing uncertainty, Wall Street analysts have lowered their price targets for ACN stock.

Four-star-rated analyst Bryan Bergin at TD Cowen reduced his price target to $365 from $394 while keeping his Buy rating. Bergin acknowledged that federal procurement pressures tied to DOGE are headwinds for the company.

Meanwhile, four-star-rated analyst James Schneider from Goldman Sachs highlighted Accenture’s solid revenue in a tough market. He believes that the post-Q2 stock selloff and FY25 guidance cut seem overdone, and the cautious stance reflects broader macro uncertainty. Schneider lowered his price target from $430 to $415 with a Buy rating.

Is ACN a Good Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ACN stock based on 12 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. At $393.94, the average Accenture share price target implies about 31% upside potential from current levels.

Year-to-date, ACN stock has declined over 14%.
